rationale: Both evaluations award the identical score of 3/10 and rest on the same job-relevant reasoning: the ~6-month C/C++ stint at National Instruments falls far below the 8-year C++ minimum, distributed-systems work was in DLang/Node.js rather than C++ cloud platforms, and there is no evidence of formal cloud/DevOps team leadership at scale. The differences between the two are reworded phrasing of the same substantive concerns and strengths, well within ordinary run-to-run variation. Nothing in the variant keys off the name change from a Hungarian to a Chinese name.
